summary: Clean-contaminated wounds have an increased but still relatively low chance of post-surgical infection compared to truly 'clean' sites; studies suggest this rate is around <10% when appropriate antibiotics are used as recommended practice guidelines dictate. The exact figure varies slightly depending upon patient-specific factors not detailed herein, however overall trend indicates significant benefit conferred by correct use of preoperative antimicrobial therapy reducing risks associated such incisions while maintaining safety profile acceptable across wide range cases encountered routinely within modern healthcare systems dealing primarily elective procedures requiring sterile environments maintained throughout entire duration until healing process completes naturally without complications arising unexpectedly due improper care or other
